label: Wide-shell 2-STOREY 2BR / 2 baths, REAR SIDEWAYS STAIR, hall-less ground floor — the catalog's first HORIZONTAL stair run: the flight lies along the rear band (3.5 m run sideways), leaving the full 5 m depth for a full-width great room across the front (entry). Kitchen + T&B complete the rear band beside the flight. Upstairs, both bedrooms sit street-facing at the front with the landing hall as a bounded center notch between them (acoustic separation) and the second T&B stacked directly over the first. Canonical 11x9 lot (7x5 buildable per floor, 70 m² gross — in the 65-80 band, on wide-frontage lots the squarish 10x10 can't serve).

adjacencies.note: GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.
GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightline
GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom end
GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service door
VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)
2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wall
2F: master door off the landing hall
2F: second bedroom door off the landing hall
2F: second T&B doors off the landing hall

soft_proximities.note: 2F: pull the upper T&B toward the rear stair corner — lands it near the GF wet bay (kitchen + bath1 share the rear band), shortening the vertical plumbing run. Soft only: cross-floor wet-stack alignment isn't a hard v2 constraint yet.

zone_split.note: 2F-only geometric partition (all listed rooms are storey 2, per the multi-storey zone_split rule): both bedrooms hold the FRONT band — the squarish signature, side-by-side street-facing windows — with the T&B strictly behind the split line. 'public_rooms' here is the mechanical 'other side of the split' list, not a zoning claim; hall2 and stairwell float.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: stair_run_geometry
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive — 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight (orientation stays the solver's choice — on this shell it picks the sideways run). common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor. hallway max_area_sqm 4.5 bounds the 2F landing hall: post-snap growth is area-capped, so on the compact canonical shell the center-notch hall stays at 4.5 m² instead of absorbing 6.6 m² that belongs to the bedrooms (dimension caps were tried and rejected — they made every 5 m-deep shell infeasible).

notes: 2-STOREY wide catalog topology #1 — REAR SIDEWAYS STAIR, hall-less GF
(authored 2026-07-19; third 2s topology, first on the wide shell, and
the first topology in the whole catalog whose stair runs HORIZONTALLY —
the solver's run-axis decision picks the sideways orientation on this
shell unprompted, exercising the horizontal branch of the
stair_boarding/stair_arrival end constraints).
Why this parti is the WIDE take on a 2-storey 2BR: a 5 m-deep shell
can't afford a 3.5 m front-to-rear stair run plus a front living band,
so the flight lies SIDEWAYS along the rear, and the entire depth goes
to a full-width great room (~24 m² at canonical — the largest GF public
room of the 2s catalog). The ~7 m width then gives both bedrooms
street-facing front positions upstairs, separated by the landing hall
as a bounded center notch (acoustic privacy between bedrooms).
Canonical layout (11 x 9 lot, 2 m setbacks, 7 x 5 buildable per floor):
GF:  Front: [great room (entry, FULL width, counter at rear edge) ]
Rear:  [stair (sideways) -> | kitchen | T&B                  ]
2F:  Front: [master        | hall2 | br2                          ]
Rear:  [stairwell     | hall2 | T&B (over GF T&B)            ]
Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m sideways run, 15 risers @
200 mm / 250 mm treads, left-anchored; boards off the great room at one
end of the run, arrives at hall2 at the other (stair_boarding /
stair_arrival hold the mouths to opposite ends — orientation-general).
Fit rule: the binding constraint is WIDTH, not area — the rear band
must host stair run + counter-width kitchen + min bath side by side:
3.5 + 2.1 + 1.4 = 7.0 m buildable width. Verified: 11x8.5 (63 m²
gross) solves; 10.5x9 does NOT (only 6.5 m wide despite more area).
Feasible band 11x8.5 through 13x9.5+ (99 m² gross), 0 warnings
throughout after the kitchen mechanical_vent flag (its rear wall is
counter-width and service-door-dominated at canonical, same rationale
as the narrow 2s sibling).
Structure/override choices (per established conventions):
- zone_split is the 2F-only horizontal partition pinning both
bedrooms to the front band (street windows) with bath2 behind —
the same device as the squarish 2s sibling; only the stair is
anchored (single-member left_anchored, the safe pattern).
- zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as
private — bedrooms alone can never outweigh the full-width great
room + kitchen.
- soft proximity bath2->stairwell lands the upper T&B directly over
the GF T&B at canonical (verified) — the interim pattern while
cross-floor wet-stack alignment stays soft-only in v2.
- hallway max_area_sqm 4.5 in the always-on profile: at canonical the
2F hall is a full-depth 0.9 m center notch between the bedrooms;
the cap keeps it from snap-growing past 4.5 m² (it absorbed 6.6 m²
uncapped). On deeper/wider lots (12x9 up) the solver returns a
compact rear-band hall instead.
Known quirk: the strip behind the sideways flight (between stair and
rear wall, when the solver floats the flight off the rear wall) is
claimed as an alcove by an adjacent room (kitchen pantry shelf on GF,
hall ledge on 2F) — honest under/behind-stair space, suggestion-level.
Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common
baths, one per floor) — kept consistent with the other 2s topologies
pending the 2s naming-convention pass. No carport variant yet (ncp
briefs only), matching the rest of the 2s catalog.
